string.text: Business Proposal
Vazgen needs the artifact %s. Just like that, no extra words or questions. A real businessman!
I’m interested in artifacts. Here’s the deal: I give you a lead on a specific artifact, and you bring it back and get a reward. Both sides are happy. What do you think?

My clients are interested in artifacts. Often, these are just “bright minds” who’ve had bad luck with the connection, or who are in possession of some science officials in the Zone. So, here’s the deal: I give you a lead on a specific artifact, and you bring it back and get a reward. Both sides are happy. So, I need the artifact %s. I’ll be waiting for it here, as will your reward.
Well done, buddy. And here are your bonuses.
Dirty Job

Vazgen has given me a dirty job - I need to find and deal with a stalker who managed to piss someone off.\n\nAll the necessary information has been provided to me via the PDA. Time to get to work. Poor guy sure got unlucky...

Sometimes, stalkers come to me when someone has wronged them, and I, in turn, find those willing to help with their dirty work so they don’t end up with problems later on. Consider me the middleman.\nRight now, I’ve got a few targets to eliminate. Usually, it’s a stalker who betrayed or sold out their friends, but sometimes there are also assassination jobs for members of other factions. It’s dirty work, but someone’s got to do it, especially if the reward is good, right? So, what do you say? I’ll send you the necessary info via PDA.
Thanks, stalker. Here’s your money, but don’t get too carried away with killing. Mercenaries don’t need competitors, either. Heh-heh...
Pseudo-bugs and Pseudo-mice

There’s a joke among stalkers: “A stalker planted a pseudo-radish. It grew into a huge pseudo-radish! The stalker took a grenade launcher and went to get it. And everything would’ve worked out if it weren’t for the pseudo-bugs and pseudo-mice.” Sounds funny, right? But sometimes these bugs and mice can be a real pain. It gets to the point where you can’t take ten steps from camp without a pseudo-something attacking you!\nThat’s why Vazgen suggested we combine business with pleasure - earn some cash by clearing the nearby areas of mutants. It’s the right thing to do, and there are too many of them around here... Plus, the trophies wouldn’t hurt either.

Tell me, have you seen any mutants on your way here? The Duty guys recently cleared the area around here, but the newly arrived stalkers have reported seeing either dogs or something else at the outer borders of the camp. If they’re not joking, the critters are a big threat both to the stalkers here and those who’ll be coming in soon. Not to mention, no clients means no business. So, I’m offering a nice reward for clearing the mutants out. Maybe I’ll even throw in something extra. The main thing is, I want them gone. Here are the approximate coordinates.
Nice work. And here are your hard-earned rewards.
Unusual Business

As they say, the rich have their quirks. It looks like one such client turned to Vazgen and asked him to find a trophy - “%s”... Vazgen, in turn, promised to pay well for delivering the trophy to him, so he could forward it to the client.
You see, I’m not only a busy man, but I’m also quite generous to those willing to help me solve my problems. And right now, I’ve got a burning order. Interested?

So, here’s the deal: one of my clients, who’s also an excellent jeweler specializing in animal trophies, wants one of those - %s. He’s willing to pay me a good amount for its delivery. As for me, I’m ready to pay you for bringing the trophy to me first. You get it, right? Make sure the mutant parts with its possession and bring it to me. You won’t be left wanting in the cash department.
Well done! Here’s your hard-earned reward.
Cache Robber
What was yours will soon be ours. Vazgen asked me to steal some important documents and deliver them to him. He sent me the coordinates on the PDA.

I’ve heard whispers about a stash with some very interesting documents. I have no idea who the owner is or where they might be now, but I’ll reward anyone who can arrange things so these documents mysteriously appear on this bar counter. Got it? I’ll send you the last known coordinates of the stash on your PDA. I doubt it’s a trap because setting someone up like that isn’t something you do to me—it's not like Sidrovich pulling off deals on the Cordon.
You really impressed me. Great job, stalker.
Mutants vs Predator

The creatures in the Zone can be quite diverse due to the emissions that drive mutants out of the center. They either gradually return, or others take their place.
But it doesn’t make it easier for anyone, especially the regular stalkers and scientists who have settled nearby.
Stalkers reported to Zmeelov that they had spotted a cluster of mutants nearby, posing a real threat to the stalkers in the area. It's time to head there and thoroughly clear the area so that the only thing left to hear is the caw of crows.

Stalkers have been here. They said mutants have settled nearby again...
It works like this: in addition to regular migration, emissions push the creatures back and forth, so we don't get to rest.
I see you’re quite experienced, and I need a hunter who can both clear the area of mutants and not disappear into thin air himself. After all, as you understand, I don’t want any blood on my hands. So, please assess your strength honestly.
So, are you up for the task?
Thank you, stalker. Thanks to you, it’s now much safer here. Here’s your reward.
Running on the Edge

Sometimes, hunting in the Zone is not just for mutants, but for stalkers as well. And it's not always an immoral activity. \nThere’s a huge difference between mercenary and bandit jobs and eliminating extremely dangerous individuals who pose a great threat to everyone. \nZmeelov received information about one such dangerous stalker, which he sent to me on the PDA. It’s time to clear the Zone of this threat to ensure the safety of other stalkers.

I'm sure you're no stranger to the danger mutants pose. However, in the Zone, a person can often be more dangerous than any mutant. 
You know... as a wise man once said, an academic, I believe: “Man is the greatest scum in the world.” 
And the Zone proves this. For example, “Monolith” and bandits—they’re the worst kind of scum; you could say they’ve had their brains washed down to the bones. But that can’t justify their actions. And there are also those individuals, who are dangerous by nature... They are just as dangerous. So, one must act decisively. 
Here’s what I’m proposing: eliminate a stalker who poses a huge threat to others. I’ll pay handsomely for it. The choice is yours. 
But I’ll say it upfront: don’t question their morality—it's too late for that.
Thank you, hunter. Here’s your reward:
A Rifle for a Hunter is Like an Axe for a Carpenter

Zmeelev is involved not only in planning operations to clear logs or gather trophies, but he also helps hunters by providing them with new weapons due to the loss of theirs or irreparable damage sustained in hand-to-hand combat with weapons.
 
Another hunter ordered “%s” from Zmeelev, and he promised to pay me well for its delivery.
 
It should be remembered that hunters, with their good deeds, make life easier for everyone in the Zone, so their weapons should be in perfect condition. At least out of respect for their work.
Have you ever had to resort to, let's say, exotic ways of dispatching game?

Experienced hunters, well, they’ve had to do it, and not just once. Sometimes, despite being in good condition, a weapon jams, and you can’t afford to delay... At that point, anything goes: spare weapons, knives, and often even the jammed barrel itself.
 
Not every hunter is capable of simply taking down a kikimora or pseudodog with a basic firearm. But it is possible. The only problem is that after such a no-holds-barred fight, the weapon is usually easier to discard than to try and fix. Especially here, where good tools are a luxury. Of course, sometimes weapons just get lost in battle...
 
Anyway, buddy, one of the hunters urgently needs a new weapon. His order is for “%s”. Ever seen one?
 
I’m willing to pay quite well for its delivery. Just keep in mind, it’s for hunters, so it must be in perfect condition. You might have issues with stalkers and factions, but betraying hunters is the last thing you want to do. After all, they make life easier for everyone here.
Alright... condition... excellent. Yes, everything’s in place and working. Good job, friend. I’ll notify the hunter about the delivery immediately.\n \nAnd here’s the money I promised you.
The Features of National Hunting

Zmeelev, one of the most famous hunters in the Shadow Valley, has given up hunting, but he’s always ready to offer his stalker brother a well-paid job related to hunting.
 As usual, Zmeelev gets the trophies he needs, and the stalker gets the money and a safe area where they can hunt for artifacts.
 
This time Zmeelev needs “%s”. A good amount of money is expected for this trophy.
I have an order for specific types of trophies. Interested?

You’ve probably already noticed that here, in the heart of the Zone, you can find creatures of every taste, size, and ferocity. The only thing they all have in common is hunger and a desire to rip apart the first stalker they see.
 
Here, I combine business with pleasure. I don’t hunt myself, but I can provide work for hunters and volunteers. The useful part is clearing the surrounding area of mutants, and the pleasant part is the trophies they bring me, along with the money I pay those who succeed in the task. I either keep the trophies for myself or send them to clients through stalkers. So, as you can see, there’s plenty to do.
 If you’re interested, I have an order right now. I need “%s” at the moment. How about it? The reward is appropriate.
Another threat has been eliminated, and the camp has been replenished with a new trophy. Wonderful. Well done, hunter.
Field of Miracles
Jorah offers an exchange: some decent equipment, according to him, in exchange for a specific artifact. He asked to find and deliver the artifact %s to him.
Wouldn’t you like to get some decent gear in exchange for an artifact?
Wouldn’t you like to get some good gear while searching for rare artifacts? I can offer you some equipment in exchange for one artifact – it’s called %s.
Great job, stalker. Here you go.
%s for Elimey
You need to find the set and take it to the medic Elimey.
I need a good set of medical tools. I can quickly patch up minor wounds, but for more serious injuries, a simple scalpel isn’t enough.
I need a good set of medical tools. I can quickly patch up minor wounds, but for more serious injuries, a simple scalpel isn’t enough.
Thank you, friend! These tools will save many lives. Thanks again for the tools.
Find %s for the Tinkerer
You need to bring tools to the Tinkerer so he can upgrade the equipment.

You know, buddy, I’m actually a pretty good technician. I can fix both weapons and armor, even make some upgrades! Well, I would, if I had proper tools. But where can I get them here? Maybe you could help me find a basic set, huh?
I need a toolset for rough work. It should include tools and oils for working with weapons. If you can get it for me, I’ll be grateful, and maybe even pay you... probably.
Great job, dude! Oh, sorry, I don’t have anything to pay you with. But I can give you a nice discount!
Find %s for the Tinkerer
You need to bring the Tinkerer tools for fine work. He needs a more complete set to perform more complex equipment upgrades.
Hey, dude, if you get me a more complete set, you’ll get an even bigger discount. Will you help me out, buddy?
I need tools for fine work. It should have everything I need. You’ll be like a brother to me if you can get it!

Wow, thanks, buddy! Now I’m definitely going to get rich. Damn, I’m going to be successful!.. If, of course, I don’t miss this chance too. But right now, I can’t offer you anything better than a discount. Sorry, buddy.
Find %s for the Tinkerer
You need to bring %s to the Tinkerer. He needs a unique set to perform even more complex equipment upgrades.
Hey, man, if you find something valuable – a discount’s on me!
I need tools for calibration. They should have everything I need. You’ll be like a brother to me if you bring them!

No way...! Damn, thanks, buddy! Now I’m definitely going to get rich. Damn, I’ll be successful!.. If, of course, I don’t miss this chance too. But right now, I can’t offer you anything better than a discount. Sorry, pal.
